title: Colymbada cyrtolepis
taxonomy: Colymbada cyrtolepis is classified within the family Asteraceae and is a species of flowering plant. It belongs to the genus Colymbada, which is a part of the subtribe Serratulinae. This species is native to the Mediterranean region, specifically in the southwestern part of the Iberian Peninsula. Colymbada cyrtolepis is closely related to other species within the subtribe, which are characterized by their similar leaf morphology and inflorescence structure. Further research is needed to fully understand the classification and relationships of this species within the Asteraceae family.
properties: Colymbada cyrtolepis, a species of plant, is reported to have various therapeutic properties. Its extracts have been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in South America, to treat digestive issues and inflammation. Additionally, studies have shown that the plant contains compounds with antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. The plant's extracts have also been investigated for their anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, making it a subject of interest for further research. Further studies are needed to confirm the efficacy of Colymbada cyrtolepis for therapeutic purposes.
constituents: Colymbada cyrtolepis contains various active constituents, including sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. These compounds have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. Research has identified specific compounds like cynaropicrin, 11-epicymaropicrin, and dihydrocynaropicrin as key components of the plant's bioactivity. The combination of these compounds may contribute to the plant's traditional uses in treating various health conditions. Further studies are needed to fully understand the effects of Colymbada cyrtolepis' active constituents on human health.
